book_claims.claim: Recommended unsweetened, calcium- and vitamin-supplemented plant milk for the Longevity Diet; source of calcium (451 mg per cup, 45% DV when fortified) and omega-3 ALA (0.1 g per 250 mL)
book_claims.mechanism: Plant-based calcium and vitamin source replacing dairy; omega-3 ALA source; choose unsweetened and supplemented with calcium, B12, B2, and D

supplementary_research.finding: Plant milk consumption (PMID 32053201) is associated with lower IGF-1 levels compared to cow's milk, which is consistent with Longo's longevity rationale for preferring plant milks over dairy
Fortified almond milk provides comparable calcium bioavailability to dairy milk in short-term studies (PMID 26561617); vitamin D fortification improves overall vitamin D status
Unfortified almond milk is very low in protein (~1 g/cup) and calories; fortification with calcium, B12, and vitamin D is essential to use it as a dairy substitute in a nutritionally complete diet

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Calcium carbonate or tricalcium phosphate typically used for fortification; shake well as calcium may settle; bioavailability comparable to dairy
Fat-soluble; the minimal fat in almond milk still aids absorption; choose D3 (cholecalciferol) fortified varieties
Naturally present from almonds; fat-soluble antioxidant
Plant form omega-3; conversion to EPA/DHA is limited; contributes modestly to overall omega-3 intake
Very low protein; not a substitute for protein-rich foods; protein-fortified versions available

synergies.description: Almond milk with oats provides plant-based calcium and fiber together; beta-glucan from oats may enhance mineral absorption
Fortified almond milk co-delivers calcium and vitamin D, optimizing calcium absorption just as dairy does
Replaces dairy creamer while avoiding IGF-1-stimulating properties of cow's milk; unsweetened version adds minimal calories
